items.title: Anthropic accuses Alibaba's Qwen team of a large-scale "distillation attack" on Claude
items.summary: Anthropic told US senators that roughly 25,000 fraudulent accounts ran 28.8 million exchanges with Claude over six weeks, allegedly to extract its reasoning and coding capabilities for training a rival model.

items.title: OpenAI unveils its first custom AI chip, "Jalapeño," built with Broadcom
items.summary: The chip is designed specifically for inference, running already-trained models to serve ChatGPT and API traffic, rather than for training new ones.

items.title: ChatGPT's share of the AI assistant market drops below half for the first time
items.summary: A widely-cited industry report puts ChatGPT at 46.4% of the global market, with Gemini climbing to 27.7% and Claude reaching 10.3%, though ChatGPT still leads on raw user count at 1.1 billion monthly users.

items.title: Meta reportedly building a wearable AI lineup for the workplace
items.summary: Beyond consumer AI glasses, Meta is said to be testing an AI-powered pendant and a business-focused line called "Wearables for Work," following its acquisition of startup Limitless.

items.title: ChatGPT reportedly passes one billion monthly active users
items.summary: The milestone underlines how quickly AI assistants have moved from novelty to daily habit for both personal and professional use.
